title: 10 best herbal decoctions for migraine
intro_desc: Herbal decoctions for migraine are concentrated liquid extracts made by steeping herbs in hot water, providing a natural relief from migraine symptoms. These decoctions work by addressing the underlying causes of migraines, such as inflammation and blood vessel constriction, while also soothing the body and mind. For example, feverfew and ginger decoctions have been shown to reduce migraine frequency and severity, improving quality of life for individuals who suffer from debilitating headaches. By incorporating these herbal remedies into their daily routine, patients can experience reduced pain and increased energy, allowing them to live more fully and without the burden of migraines.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Feverfew decoctions helps with migraine because of its unique ability to reduce inflammation and relax muscle contractions. The parthenolide compound found in feverfew has a natural analgesic effect, which helps alleviate headache pain and pressure. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of feverfew decoction can help constrict blood vessels, reducing dilation that often contributes to migraine attacks. By targeting both pain and inflammation, herbal feverfew decoctions provide effective relief for individuals suffering from migraines.
remedy_properties: Parthenolide content: Feverfew contains parthenolide, a sesquiterpene lactone that helps to relax blood vessels and reduce inflammation, which can contribute to migraine pain.

Anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects: The decoction's anti-inflammatory properties can help to reduce swelling and pain in the head and neck, while its analgesic properties can help to alleviate headache symptoms.

Cytokine-modulating activity: Feverfew has been shown to modulate the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, which are involved in the pathophysiology of migraines, helping to reduce inflammation and alleviate migraine symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Camphor: It helps with migraine by acting as a vasodilator, which means it widens blood vessels and improves blood flow, potentially reducing the severity of migraine symptoms.
Thujone: It helps with migraine by exerting a sedative effect on the nervous system, which may help reduce the frequency and severity of migraine attacks.

Sesquiterpene lactones (including parthenolide): They help with migraine by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and mediators, which may contribute to the development of migraine symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Tanacetum parthenium are commonly used due to their high concentration of feverfew's active compounds, which help in preventing migraine headaches.

Flowers: The flowers of Tanacetum parthenium are widely used as they contain sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties that help in alleviating migraine symptoms.

Buds: The buds of Tanacetum parthenium are also utilized in migraine decoctions as they contain a high amount of sesquiterpene lactones, which contribute to their anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried tanacetum parthenium flowers and leaves from a clean and dry area.
Combine the dried tanacetum parthenium with 1 liter of boiling water in a clean glass container.
Reduce heat to a simmer and allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Allow the decoction to cool down and then store it in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger decoctions helps with migraine because of its unique combination of anti-inflammatory compounds, including gingerols and shogaols. These bioactive molecules have been shown to inhibit the production of prostaglandins, which are hormones that cause blood vessels to dilate and contribute to migraine pain. Ginger decoctions may also help relax muscles, reduce inflammation, and improve circulation, all of which can help alleviate migraine symptoms. Additionally, ginger has natural analgesic properties that can help ease headache pain, providing relief from debilitating migraines.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the body, which is a common underlying cause of migraine pain.

Pain-relieving volatile oils: The volatile oils present in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, have analgesic and anti-pyretic properties that help relieve migraine headaches by blocking pain transmission pathways.
Antioxidant activity: Ginger's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals in the body, which can contribute to oxidative stress and inflammation that may trigger or exacerbate migraines.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds, specifically 6-gingerol and 8-gingerol, have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and inflammation associated with migraines.
Shogaols: These compounds, specifically 6-shogaol and 8-shogaol, have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which help alleviate migraine pain and reduce inflammation in blood vessels.

Zingiberene: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which are contributing factors to migraine development and severity.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of active compounds such as gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Roots: Roots of Zingiber officinale are also widely used as they contain similar active compounds as rhyzomes, making them effective in reducing migraine symptoms.
Stems: Stems are sometimes used as a substitute for rhyzomes and roots, although they may have lower concentrations of active compounds, they can still provide some relief from migraine symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1 to 2 teaspoons of dried zingiber officinale root.
Combine the measured zingiber officinale root with 1 quart of boiling water in a saucepan.
Reduce the heat to a simmer and let the decoction steep for 5 to 10 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a large bowl.
Discard the solids and let the decoction cool to room temperature for later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White willow decoctions helps with migraine because they contain salicin, a natural compound similar to aspirin. Salicin is able to relieve pain by blocking the production of certain chemicals in the brain that cause inflammation and lead to throbbing headaches. The decoction's anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce swelling in blood vessels, which can contribute to migraine symptoms. By targeting the root causes of migraine pain, white willow decoctions provide a natural and effective way to alleviate discomfort and restore well-being.
remedy_properties: Salicin content: White willow contains salicin, a natural pain reliever similar to aspirin, which helps to reduce migraine pain by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, thereby decreasing inflammation and swelling in the blood vessels.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The decoction's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce inflammation in the trigeminal nerve, which is often triggered during a migraine episode, alleviating symptoms such as throbbing headaches and facial pain.

Antispasmodic activity: White willow's antispasmodic properties help to relax blood vessels and reduce muscle spasms in the neck and shoulders, which are common areas of tension that can exacerbate migraines.
remedy_constituents: Salicin: Acts as a natural pain reliever due to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce migraine pain and inflammation.
Quercetin: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce migraine severity by alleviating inflammation and oxidative stress in the body.
Salicortin: Has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help relax blood vessels and reduce migraine-related pain and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Barks: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Leaves: They are used due to their potential to reduce pain and inflammation associated with migraines.
Buds: They are used due to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate migraine symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salix alba bark or dried white willow bark in quantities of 20-50 grams per liter of water.
Chop the harvested bark into smaller pieces to increase the surface area for infusion.
Combine the chopped bark with 1 liter of cold water in a non-reactive pot or container.
Bring the mixture to a boil and then reduce heat to a simmer for 10-15 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container to remove the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint decoctions helps with migraine because of its natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The menthol content in peppermint relaxes the muscles, reduces tension headaches, and soothes the digestive system, which can often be a trigger for migraines. Additionally, peppermint's calming effects on the mind and body may help alleviate stress, anxiety, and emotional triggers that can exacerbate migraine symptoms. As a result, herbal peppermint decoctions can provide natural relief from migraine pain and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as menthol and menthone, help reduce inflammation in blood vessels, which can contribute to migraine pain.
Pain-relieving properties: The analgesic and antispasmodic properties of peppermint oil may help alleviate headache pain by blocking the production of pain-causing chemicals in the brain.
Gastroprotective properties: Peppermint's anti-inflammatory compounds may also help soothe gastrointestinal issues, such as nausea and vomiting, which are common symptoms of migraines.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene has anti-inflammatory and neuroprotective properties, which can help alleviate migraine pain and reduce inflammation in the brain.
Roscavin: A flavonoid component of mint, roscavin has antioxidant properties that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with migraine.
Menthone: A monoterpene ketone, menthone has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help relieve migraine pain and reduce inflammation in the body.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in menthol, which has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate migraine symptoms.
Stems: The stems of Mentha x piperita contain menthol and other compounds that can help ease pain and reduce inflammation associated with migraines.
Roots: Mentha x piperita roots contain menthone, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce migraine pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh mentha x piperita leaves and stems in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Chop 20-30 grams of fresh mentha x piperita leaves and stems into small pieces for easier infusion.
Combine the chopped mentha x piperita with 250 milliliters of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes then strain it using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve.
Discard the solids and let the decoction cool to room temperature before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop decoctions helps with migraine because its potent combination of flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins work synergistically to reduce inflammation and relieve tension in blood vessels. The anti-inflammatory properties of maypop help to decrease the pain and discomfort associated with migraines, while its sedative effects calm the nervous system and promote relaxation. Additionally, maypop's ability to improve circulation helps to increase oxygenation of the brain, further alleviating migraine symptoms. As a natural and non-pharmacological remedy, maypop decoctions offer a safe and effective solution for managing migraines.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Maypop decoctions contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which can help reduce inflammation and swelling in blood vessels associated with migraines.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in maypop decoctions, such as flavonoids and anthocyanins, can neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress, which is believed to play a role in the development of migraine headaches.

Pain-relieving and anti-nociceptive effects: Maypop contains compounds like flavonoids and glycosides, which have been shown to have pain-relieving and anti-nociceptive properties, helping to reduce migraine-related pain and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Isophorone: Isophorone is a sesquiterpene that contributes to the anxiolytic and anti-migraine properties of Passiflora incarnata by modulating neurotransmitter activity and reducing stress-induced migraine triggers.

Passifloric acid: Passifloric acid, a phenolic compound, has been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate migraine symptoms by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain.

Harman and norharman: Harman and norharman, a pair of indole alkaloids, have been found to possess sedative and antispasmodic properties, which may help alleviate migraine symptoms by reducing muscle tension and promoting relaxation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used to make decoctions due to their high concentration of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that help alleviate migraine symptoms.
Roots: Utilized for their sedative properties, which help to reduce inflammation and ease migraine pain.
Fruits: Employed in decoctions due to their high content of passifloric acid, a compound with anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that aid in migraine relief.
remedy_recipe: Gather 4 to 8 ounces of fresh or dried passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves for decoction preparation.
Combine the gathered flowers and leaves with 1 quart of boiling water in a large pot.
Reduce heat to a simmer and steep the mixture for 10 to 15 minutes to release active compounds.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a bowl to separate solids from liquid.
Store the resulting decoction in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian decoctions helps with migraine because of its unique ability to relax muscles, calm the nervous system, and reduce anxiety. The aromatic compounds in valerian root have a sedative effect on the brain, helping to ease tension headaches and alleviate migraine symptoms. By promoting relaxation and reducing stress, valerian decoctions can also help regulate sleep patterns, which is often disrupted during migraines. This natural remedy has been shown to provide relief from migraine pain, frequency, and duration, making it a valuable addition to any migraine management plan.
remedy_properties: Sedative and relaxing effects: Valerian's sedative properties can help calm the nervous system, reducing tension and stress that can trigger or exacerbate migraines.

Pain relief: Valerian contains compounds like isovaleric acid and valerenic acid, which have been shown to exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to alleviate migraine pain and discomfort.

Muscle relaxant properties: Valerian's ability to relax muscles can help reduce muscle tension in the neck and head, a common trigger for migraines, and may also help alleviate associated symptoms like headaches and facial pain.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: Valerenic acid has been shown to have a sedative effect, which can help alleviate migraine symptoms such as pain and anxiety by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation.

Isovaleric acid: Isovaleric acid, a valerenic acid derivative, has been found to have a neuroprotective effect, which can help prevent migraine attacks by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain.
Valeranone: Valeranone has been shown to have a sedative and anxiolytic effect, which can help reduce the frequency and severity of migraine attacks by promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian root is the most commonly used part due to its high concentration of valerenic acids, which have a sedative effect on the nervous system and can help alleviate migraine symptoms.
Seeds: Valerian seeds are used for their ability to stimulate the body's natural response to pain and inflammation, which can help reduce migraine severity and duration.
Leaves: Valerian leaves are sometimes used for their antioxidant properties, which can help protect the body from oxidative stress and inflammation that may contribute to migraine development.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 5 to 10 grams of dried valeriana officinalis roots in late summer or early autumn.
Rinse the valeriana roots in cold water to remove any impurities and debris.
Combine the valeriana roots with 250 milliliters of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Steep the mixture for 5 to 7 minutes allowing the active compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the decoction using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove the valeriana roots.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender decoctions helps with migraine because of its calming properties, which can ease tension and relaxation. The floral fragrance of lavender has been shown to reduce anxiety and stress, two common triggers of migraines. Additionally, lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds may help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with the condition. By drinking a warm decoction made from dried English lavender flowers, individuals can experience relief from migraine symptoms, promoting a sense of calm and well-being.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant: Lavender's antioxidant properties can help reduce oxidative stress, which is thought to contribute to the development of migraines, by neutralizing free radicals and reducing inflammation.

Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lavender, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, may help reduce swelling and pain associated with migraines, providing relief from symptoms like throbbing headaches and sensitivity to light.

Anxiolytic and Sedative: Lavender's calming effects can help reduce stress and anxiety, common migraine triggers, by promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension, which can contribute to the development of migraines.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene acts as an anti-inflammatory and analgesic, helping to reduce pain and inflammation associated with migraines.
Linalyl acetate: A terpene that has a sedative effect, which can help alleviate migraine symptoms by promoting relaxation and reducing stress.

Luteolin: A flavonoid phenolic compound with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation that may contribute to migraine development and severity.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their calming and analgesic properties, which help alleviate migraine pain and reduce inflammation.
Flowers: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and calming effects, which can help reduce migraine severity and promote relaxation.
Stems: They are used due to their potential for containing calming and analgesic compounds that may help alleviate migraine pain and symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in the early morning to ensure optimal potency.
Measure 2 teaspoons of dried flowers or 4 teaspoons of fresh flowers and place them in a heat-resistant cup.
Boil 1 cup of water and pour it over the lavandula angustifolia flowers in the cup.
Allow the decoction to steep for 5 to 10 minutes to release the herbal compounds into the water.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean cup to remove the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric decoctions helps with migraine because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the blood vessels and nerves that can contribute to migraine symptoms. The decoction's curcumin content also has a natural analgesic effect, providing relief from pain and discomfort associated with migraines. Additionally, turmeric's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that may exacerbate migraine triggers. By addressing these underlying causes, herbal turmeric decoctions offer a natural and effective way to manage migraine symptoms.
remedy_properties: Cyclolic Acid: This property helps to reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, which can contribute to migraine pain by relaxing the vessel walls and improving blood flow.
Curcuminoids: These powerful antioxidants have potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help to block pain pathways in the brain, reducing the severity of migraine attacks.

Volatile Oils: The volatile oils present in turmeric decoctions, such as curcumene and terpenes, have a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help to calm nervous tension and reduce migraine triggers.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Curcumin, a polyphenolic compound, helps with migraine by inhibiting pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes, thereby reducing inflammation and pain associated with migraines.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): Demethoxycurcumin, another polyphenolic compound, helps with migraine by blocking the production of pro-inflammatory molecules and preventing the activation of pain-sensing neurons, thus providing relief from migraine symptoms.

Caryophyllene: Caryophyllene, a sesquiterpene, helps with migraine by acting as an anti-inflammatory and neuroprotective agent, reducing oxidative stress and preventing the activation of pain-sensing neurons, ultimately providing relief from migraine symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in curcuminoids, which have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
Roots: Contain active compounds that help in reducing inflammation and alleviating migraine symptoms.
Barks: May contain bioactive compounds with analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects that help in managing migraine pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rhizomes of curcuma longa at the right time when they are mature and dry.
Clean the rhizomes thoroughly under running water to remove any impurities and dirt.
Slice the rhizomes into small pieces of about 2 to 3 centimeters in length to increase surface area.
Boil the sliced rhizomes in 1 liter of water for about 30 to 40 minutes to extract active compounds.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine mesh to obtain a clear liquid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion decoctions helps with migraine because it is a natural diuretic that can help to reduce water retention, which is often associated with migraines. The decoction's anti-inflammatory properties can also help to alleviate pain and swelling in the blood vessels of the brain. Additionally, dandelion's magnesium content can help to relax tense muscles and improve circulation, further reducing migraine symptoms. By addressing these underlying causes, herbal dandelion decoctions may provide relief from migraines without harsh chemicals or prescription medications.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as taraxasterol and triterpenoids, can help reduce inflammation in the blood vessels and nerves, which is thought to contribute to migraine symptoms.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of dandelion decoctions may help neutralize free radicals that can trigger oxidative stress and damage to brain cells, which can exacerbate migraine symptoms.

Soothing digestive properties: Dandelion's bitter compounds have been shown to stimulate digestion, alleviate constipation, and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to migraines by releasing toxins and irritating nerve endings.
remedy_constituents: Inulin: Inulin acts as a natural anti-inflammatory agent, helping to reduce inflammation and pain associated with migraines.

Phenolic acids (specifically, caffeic acid and ferulic acid): Phenolic acids have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and vasoconstrictive properties, which can help reduce migraine severity and frequency by inhibiting pain transmission and improving blood vessel function.

Taraxasterol (a sesquiterpene lactone): Taraxasterol has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate migraine symptoms by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Their volatile oils and flavonoids are believed to help alleviate migraine symptoms by reducing pain and inflammation.
Flowers: They contain flavonoids and volatile oils that contribute to their potential anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
Roots: The roots of Taraxacum officinale are thought to have a sedative effect, which may help in reducing migraine severity and frequency.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh taraxacum officinale roots and leaves for medicinal use in late spring to early summer.
Chop 10-30 grams of the roots and leaves into smaller pieces for increased surface area.
Combine the chopped roots and leaves with 500 ml of water in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to a simmer for 10-15 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container to remove solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary decoctions helps with migraine because it contains compounds that have a calming effect on the mind and body. The herb's essential oils, such as camphor and borneol, help to reduce inflammation in the blood vessels of the brain, which can contribute to migraine pain. Additionally, rosemary's antioxidant properties may help to neutralize free radicals that can trigger migraines. By relaxing muscle tension and reducing inflammation, rosemary decoctions can provide relief from migraine symptoms, including headache pain, sensitivity to light, and nausea.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Rosemary's antispasmodic property helps to relax muscle tension, which is a common migraine trigger, and reduces spasms in blood vessels, alleviating headache pain.

Anti-inflammatory property: Rosemary's anti-inflammatory properties reduce inflammation in the brain, which can contribute to migraine development and severity, and also help alleviate related symptoms such as sensitivity to light and sound.

Antioxidant property: Rosemary's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can damage brain cells and contribute to migraines, helping to protect against oxidative stress and promote overall brain health.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound that helps with migraine by exerting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which are associated with migraine development.

Carnosic acid: A phenolic diterpene that helps with migraine by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes, thereby reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain and alleviating migraine symptoms.

α-Pinene: A terpene that helps with migraine by acting as an analgesic and anti-inflammatory agent, reducing pain sensitivity and inflammation in the brain, and also by modulating the activity of neurotransmitters involved in pain perception.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high concentration of essential oils, particularly camphor and bornyl acetate, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Stems: They are utilized for their similar composition to leaves, with a high content of essential oils, which can provide relief from migraine symptoms.
Flowers: They are used due to their calming and sedative effects, which can help alleviate migraine headaches and other symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1 teaspoon of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves for every 8 ounces of water.
Combine measured rosmarinus officinalis with 8 ounces of water in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over medium heat for 5 to 10 minutes or until the liquid is hot.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove the solids.
Allow the decoction to cool to room temperature before consuming the liquid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree decoctions helps with migraine because it possesses anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties that effectively alleviate headache symptoms. The decoction's active compounds, such as flavonoids and triterpenes, work to constrict blood vessels, reducing the severity of pain and discomfort associated with migraines. Additionally, its calming effects help to regulate neurotransmitters, further alleviating migraine-related anxiety and stress. As a natural remedy, maidenhair tree decoctions offer a safe and effective way to manage migraine symptoms without relying on pharmaceuticals.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in blood vessels, which can contribute to migraine pain.
Vasodilatory effects: Ginkgo biloba's ability to dilate blood vessels may help improve blood flow to the brain and reduce migraines by reducing vessel constriction and subsequent pain.

Calcitonin gene-related peptide (CGRP) inhibition: Ginkgo biloba has been shown to inhibit the release of CGRP, a neurotransmitter involved in migraine pathophysiology, which may help reduce migraine frequency and severity.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Ginkgo biloba contain bioactive compounds like ginkgolides and bilobalide, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in reducing migraine pain.
Barks: The barks of the Maidenhair Tree contain flavonoids and terpenes that have been found to possess analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to alleviate migraine symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginkgo biloba leaves from a trusted source or purchase dried leaf extract in capsules.
Grind the ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine 10-15 grams of the ground ginkgo biloba with 500ml of boiling water in a glass container.
Simmer the mixture for 30 minutes to extract the active compounds from the herb.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle and store.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage decoctions helps with migraine because they possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which effectively alleviate the intense pain and inflammation associated with migraines. The essential oils present in sage leaves, such as camphor, borneol, and thujone, work together to relax tense muscles, reduce blood vessel constriction, and calm the nervous system, providing relief from migraine symptoms. Additionally, sage's anti-inflammatory compounds help to decrease prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that can trigger migraines. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, sage decoctions provide a natural and effective solution for managing migraine attacks.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, can help reduce inflammation and swelling in blood vessels, which may contribute to migraine pain.
Antioxidant properties: Sage's antioxidants, like carnosic acid, can neutralize free radicals that can damage brain cells and contribute to oxidative stress, a known trigger for migraines.

Vasodilatory properties: The flavonoids present in sage, such as quercetin, can help relax blood vessels and improve blood flow, which may help alleviate migraine symptoms by reducing blood vessel constriction.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots have been traditionally used to relieve tension and ease migraine pain due to their calming effects on the nervous system.
Barks: The barks of the sage plant contain flavonoids, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate migraine symptoms such as headaches.
remedy_recipe: Harvest one tablespoon of fresh or dried salvia officinalis leaves and flowers.
Combine the harvested herb with two cups of boiling water in a large glass bowl.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes, then strain it through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean cup.
Allow the decoction to cool to room temperature before refrigerating or freezing it for up to three days.
Consume one-quarter cup of the cooled decoction as needed up to three times daily to help alleviate migraine symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm decoctions helps with migraine because it has a profound effect on reducing anxiety and stress, common triggers of migraines. The calming properties of lemon balm also help to ease tension headaches by relaxing muscles and improving sleep quality. Additionally, lemon balm's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, which can contribute to migraine pain. By incorporating lemon balm decoctions into one's routine, individuals can experience a significant reduction in migraine frequency and severity.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Lemon balm helps to relax tense muscles, particularly those involved in migraine pain, thereby reducing the severity and frequency of spasms that can contribute to migraine symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory property: Lemon balm has anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce inflammation in the blood vessels and trigeminal nerve, which are thought to play a role in migraine development, thus alleviating pain and discomfort.

Antioxidant property: Lemon balm's antioxidant activity helps to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress, which is believed to be involved in the development of migraines, thereby reducing the risk of migraines and their severity.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that can help alleviate migraine symptoms.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids and terpenes that have a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing stress and tension that can trigger migraines.
remedy_recipe: Measure out one tablespoon of dried melissa officinalis leaves or flowers.
Combine the measured herbs with one cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass bowl.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes, covered with a lid or plate.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container.
Store the cooled decoction in an airtight container and consume one-half cup three times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile decoctions helps with migraine because of its calming and soothing properties, which can effectively reduce the frequency and severity of migraines. The apigenin present in chamomile binds to GABA receptors, promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation in the brain. This natural remedy also has anti-anxiety and sedative effects, helping to alleviate stress-induced migraines. By sipping on a warm cup of chamomile decoction, individuals can find relief from migraine symptoms and promote overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help reduce inflammation in blood vessels, which can contribute to migraine pain.
Sedative and anxiolytic property: Chamomile's calming effects can help alleviate anxiety and stress, which are common migraine triggers, and promote relaxation to reduce tension headache symptoms.
Antispasmodic property: Chamomile's antispasmodic compounds, such as matricin, help relax smooth muscle in blood vessels, reducing spasms and constrictions that can lead to migraine pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in apigenin, a flavonoid that helps to relax muscles and reduce inflammation, which can provide relief from migraine symptoms.
Flowers: Containing anti-inflammatory compounds like bisabolol, they help to soothe headaches and calm the nervous system.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2 teaspoons of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers into a clean glass container.
Boil 2 cups of water in a saucepan and pour it over the herb mixture.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes before straining and discarding solids.
Allow the decoction to cool down to room temperature before transferring it to an amber glass bottle.
Store the final product in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder decoctions helps with migraine because of its unique ability to relax and soothe the nervous system, reducing inflammation and pain. The bioactive compounds present in elderflowers and leaves have a sedative effect on the brain, calming down excitable neurons that can trigger migraines. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Elder decoction has been shown to reduce inflammation, which can contribute to migraines by helping to relax blood vessels and reduce pain.

Antioxidant properties: Elder's antioxidant compounds help protect against oxidative stress, a known trigger for migraine episodes, and also have anti-vasoconstrictive effects that can alleviate migraine symptoms.
Vasodilatory properties: The flavonoids and phenolic acids in elder decoction can dilate blood vessels, which helps to reduce vasospasm and alleviate migraine pain.

remedy_recipe: Harvest dried sambucus nigra flowers one ounce per cup of water.
Combine the harvested herbs with cold filtered water in a clean pot.
Boil the mixture for 10-15 minutes or until the liquid has reduced slightly.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a large bowl.
Allow the decoction to cool and then store it in an amber glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme decoctions helps with migraine because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce pain and swelling in the brain. remedy_recipe: Gather 20-30 grams of dried thymus vulgaris leaves and stems for the decoction.
Measure out one liter of distilled water and bring it to a boil in a medium-sized pot.
Add the dried thymus vulgaris mixture to the boiling water and reduce heat to a simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a separate container, discarding the solids after 30-45 minutes of steeping time.
Store the resulting decoction in an amber glass bottle at room temperature for up to 3 days before use or refrigerate for up to one week.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon decoctions helps with migraine because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. Rhizomes: Contain essential oils that provide analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties to relieve migraine symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 grams of dried cinnamomum verum bark from a trusted source and wash it gently under cold running water.
Grind the bark into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for about 30 seconds until it reaches a consistent texture.
Measure out 1 gram of the powdered bark and add it to 200 milliliters of boiling filtered water in a glass teapot or saucepan.
Reduce heat to low and simmer the mixture for 10-15 minutes, allowing the active compounds to infuse into the water, resulting in a rich brown liquid.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass container and let it cool to room temperature before refrigerating or consuming as needed within 24 hours.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh borago officinalis leaves in early morning or late afternoon for optimal potency.
Rinse 10-15 grams of the harvested leaves gently with cold running water to remove impurities.
Chop the rinsed leaves coarsely and transfer them to a large glass container.
Pour 500ml of boiling distilled water over the chopped leaves, cover the container, and let it steep for 20-30 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and store in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried centella asiatica leaves and flowers for every 250 milliliters of water needed.
Combine the gathered centella asiatica with boiling water in a pot for 10-15 minutes steeping time.
Allow the mixture to cool down to room temperature before straining it using cheesecloth or a coffee filter.
Mix one teaspoon of honey and 1/2 teaspoon of ginseng powder into the cooled decoction for added sweetness and medicinal benefits.
Refrigerate the prepared decoction in an airtight container for up to three days and consume two tablespoons twice daily as needed.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to migraine that are treated with herbal decoctions are tension headaches, sinusitis, and cluster headaches. Decoctions made from herbs such as feverfew, ginger, and willow bark can help alleviate symptoms like pain, inflammation, and congestion. Additionally, herbal remedies like passionflower and valerian root may be used to treat anxiety and stress-related headache disorders. These natural treatments offer a safer alternative to pharmaceuticals for many individuals seeking relief from recurring headaches.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal decoctions that help with migraine is a blend of feverfew, ginger, and passionflower. Feverfew contains a compound called parthenolide, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce migraine frequency and severity. Ginger's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties can help alleviate pain and nausea, while passionflower's calming effects can help reduce anxiety and stress, common migraine triggers. This combination can be brewed as a tea or taken in supplement form to provide relief. Consult a healthcare professional before using.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Phytotherapy research : PTR, herbal decoctions are beneficial for migraine due to the positive, albeit limited evidence found for butterbur and preliminary findings on curcumin, citron, and coriander as a prophylactic treatment. The study conducted a systematic literature review of 19 randomised, controlled human trials assessing the effects of herbal treatments on migraine, with a focus on single-ingredient treatments. The results of this review suggest that several herbal medicines, such as feverfew, butterbur, curcumin, menthol/peppermint oil, coriander, citron, and chamomile, present as potential options to enhance the treatment of migraine, although further high-quality research is necessary to confirm their efficacy and safety.
